title: Cookies, Credential Modes, API Keys, Bearer Tokens, and Secret Hygiene · Go Client Systems · Go Semantics Case: Credential Provider Design
summary: A multi-tenant operator client rotates short-lived bearer tokens and isolated cookie jars without logging or forwarding credentials. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http go credential provider design increment in a Go 1.26 net/http least-authority credential and session policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Inject short-lived credentials through a testable provider with rotation, concurrency, redaction, and failure policy. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Acquire, attach, scope, refresh, expire, and revoke bearer tokens without treating possession as identity proof. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go Predict Credential Provider Design · Theory Credential Provider Design
steps.instruction:
theory case — Go fleet evidence model handles host-only session cookie SID-6, SameSite policy, API key, and expiring bearer token while two accounts run concurrently. Define jar isolation, CSRF, scope, rotation, redaction, and redirect boundaries. Competency probe: Inject short-lived credentials through a testable provider with rotation, concurrency, redaction, and failure policy. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Credential Provider Design contract for the supplied data.

For Go Predict Credential Provider Design · Theory Credential Provider Design, keep Credential Provider Design connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.
